A.reason: It identifies someone who does the same thing again after an earlier doing of it.

B.missing: It requires many later repetitions, whereas repeating once is sufficient; it carries an over-narrow repeated-many-times subtype.
B.reason: The same-action recurrence is present, but the added many-times condition wrongly narrows the sense.

C.reason: It states that someone does one thing and does that same thing again later.

D.reason: It entails that the person does the same thing before and again at the stated time, though redundantly.

E.reason: It identifies someone who does the same thing again after an earlier doing of it.
